channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gujarat to set up India’s third satellite launch center near Gir Somnath
item.description: The Gujarat government has announced plans for India’s third satellite launch center, which will be located near Gir Somnath along the Arabian Sea. The site has been selected after consultations with ...

item.title: Omugo Community Condemns Abduction of Eight Worshippers at ECWA Church in Kwara, Calls for Urgent Rescue
item.description: The Omugo Development Union (ODU) don express deep sorrow and strong condemnation over the barbaric abduction of eight innocent worshippers at ECWA Church, Omugo in Oro Ago District of Ifelodun Local ...

item.title: The notwithstanding clause as protection against populism in Canada
item.description: In the debate over Quebec's Bill 21, which prohibits certain public sector workers from wearing religious symbols, legal experts have turned their attention to Canada's use of the notwithstanding clau...

item.title: House of Representatives Begins Impeachment Hearings for VP Sara Duterte
item.description: The House of Representatives on March 25, 2026, began the official hearings on the impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte. The hearing will cover various motions, subpoenas, and wi...

item.title: Rajasthan Villagers Protest to Protect Sacred Groves and Pastures from Large Solar Parks
item.description: In Rajasthan, the traditional oases of the desert known as orans, which are sacred groves managed by local communities, are under threat from large-scale solar power projects. These areas are crucial ...

item.title: Petrol Prices Soar Amid Fuel Supply Crisis and Global Tensions
item.description: Petrol prices in Australia have reached a record high average of $2.38 per litre, as the country faces a looming fuel supply crisis. According to reports, the crisis is set to worsen as Australian ref...

item.title: Local incident: Dog beaten to death, NGO volunteers face hostility while trying to help
item.description: In a disturbing incident reported recently, a man physically assaulted a dog, resulting in its death, in a local area. The attempt by NGO workers to intervene and save the animal was met with aggressi...

item.title: New Mexico Jury Orders Meta to Pay $375 Million for Violating Consumer Protection Laws Related to Child Safety
item.description: A New Mexico jury has ruled that Meta Platforms violated state consumer protection laws by misleading users about the safety of Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, and by enabling child sexual exploita...

item.title: Makati City Government Signs Renewable Energy Deal with ACEN Corp.
item.description: Amid the global oil crisis, Makati city's facilities are now powered by renewable energy. The city's 154 properties, including city hall, Ospital ng Makati, and University of Makati, will source elect...

item.title: Two Jazz Festivals in the Western Cape Could Boost Collaboration and Creativity
item.description: As the Cape Town International Jazz Festival (CTIJF) and the Montreux Jazz Festival stage events in the Western Cape on the same weekend, initial concerns about competition are giving way to recogniti...

item.title: Ottawa Proposes New Rule to Protect Southern Resident Orcas from Vessel Disturbance
item.description: Ottawa has introduced a proposal to protect southern resident orcas from vessels by mandating a one-kilometre distance between ships and these endangered whales. This measure is aimed at reducing dist...

item.title: Norbert Ndjeka: The doctor who transformed South Africa’s fight against drug-resistant TB
item.description: Dr Norbert Ndjeka, born on 24 March 1965 in the Democratic Republic of the Congo — coinciding with World TB Day — became one of South Africa’s leading figures in the battle against tuberculosis. From ...

item.title: Capital Brewing founders open Lager House pub in Canberra's Inner North
item.description: The founders of Capital Brewing in Fyshwick, Laurence Kain and Tom Hertel, are set to launch a new pub called Lager House, which is inspired by a unique customer love story. The pub, located in a new ...

item.title: UK government to trial social media restrictions on teenagers including full bans, time limits and overnight curfews
item.description: The UK government is launching a pilot scheme involving 300 teenagers to test various forms of social media restrictions in their homes. Participants will be divided into four groups: one where popula...

item.title: Nigerian Army apprehends suspected ammunition courier in Kogi State
item.description: Nigerian Army troops don arrest one person wey dem dey suspect say e dey carry ammunition for Kogi State. The operation happen for Ahokon, Koton Karfe, around 6:30 p.m on weekend after security agents...

item.title: Government must urgently fix underfunded public hospitals and restore trust in Kenya's health system
item.description: The article argues that fixing Kenya's public hospitals should be the top priority for the government, given how badly they are currently underfunded and understaffed. The writer points out that many ...

item.title: New Brunswick seeks company to reopen historical antimony mine
item.description: The government of New Brunswick is on the lookout for a company to restart a critical mineral mine, which was once one of the largest producers of antimony in North America. Located about 30 kilometre...

item.title: Tanzania Government Finalises Movable Assets as Collateral for Bank Loans to Boost Credit Access
item.description: The Tanzanian government is in the final stages of rolling out a new system that will let citizens use movable assets like vehicles, equipment, livestock or even inventory as collateral when seeking l...

item.title: How to Fairly Divide Assets After a Creative Partnership Ends
item.description: When a creative partnership dissolves, the division of assets can become complex. The first step is to identify what counts as a shared asset—this includes physical property like equipment and digital...

item.title: Kenyan education stakeholders urge government to prioritise quality over mere access
item.description: The article reports that education stakeholders in Kenya are calling on the government to move beyond simply increasing access to schooling and instead focus on improving the quality of education. The...
